title: Question 1.1
statement_text: For which graphs $F$ is there an $F$-counting lemma in $C_4$-free graphs?
context_text: This is the central open problem of the paper. The authors formally define what it means for a graph $F$ to be 'countable' (Definition 1.3) and construct infinite families of countable graphs, but a complete characterization remains open. They note that a necessary condition is that $F$ has girth at least 5 (Remark 1.6).
notes: Labeled '(Main question, informal)' in the paper; the informal qualifier refers to the prose level of precision, not the environment type.
paper_title: Which graphs can be counted in $C_4$-free graphs?

title: Open Problem 2.17
statement_text: Are the dodecahedral and Petersen graphs countable?
context_text: After constructing many countable graphs of girth at least 5 via a recursive 'islands and bridges' method, the authors observe that their techniques appear insufficient to handle 3-regular graphs of girth at least 5. The dodecahedral graph (girth 5, 3-regular) and the Petersen graph (girth 5, 3-regular) are highlighted as the simplest open cases.
notes: PDF source — figures of the two graphs referenced in the problem statement are not recoverable from the text extraction.
paper_title: Which graphs can be counted in $C_4$-free graphs?
